title: 10 best herbal creams for eye irritation

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidants: Aloe vera's antioxidants, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eye, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision.

Soothing humectants: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes by creating a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, calming discomfort and reducing tear film instability.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with eye irritation because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel-like substance extracted from the aloe plant has been used for centuries to calm and moisturize the skin. When applied around the eyes, aloe vera cream can reduce redness and swelling, providing instant relief from irritation caused by allergies, dryness, or exposure to chemicals. Its natural cooling effect also helps to decongest and comfort the eyes, promoting a sense of calm and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloin acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling associated with eye irritation.
Apolypodol: Apolypodol exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to eye irritation.
Aloin B: Aloin B has antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes and exacerbate eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Gel from Leaves: Provides anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Sap from Leaves: Contains a high concentration of aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help protect and soothe the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel and mix it with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a large bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the bowl and heat gently until melted.
Remove the bowl from heat and stir in 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.
Remove the jar from the refrigerator and give the cream a good stir before transferring it to a smaller jar for use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation, providing quick relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals in the eyes, which can contribute to irritation and damage, promoting a healthier ocular environment.

Calming and soothing: Chamomile's calming effects help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate eye irritation, while also providing a gentle, non-irritating barrier for dry, itchy, or red eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds present in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The cream's ability to calm and quiet the skin can provide quick relief from eye irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or irritants. By applying the cream to the affected area, individuals can experience a reduction in discomfort and an improvement in their overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling associated with eye irritation.
α-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to calm and protect the eyes from further irritation.
Luteolin: This flavonoid exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes caused by irritation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are widely used in creams for eye irritation due to their high content of chamazulene and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used in creams for eye irritation due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them a valuable ingredient in soothing eye creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of matricaria chamomilla at full bloom when they have a strong apple-like fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm well-ventilated area with low humidity for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse the dried flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine the infused oil with a emulsifier such as beeswax and a preservative in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like luteolin and apigenin, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes caused by irritants or allergies.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to irritation.
Soilng and moisturizing properties: The soothing and moisturizing effects of pot marigold cream help calm dry, itchy, or irritated eyes by locking in moisture and reducing redness and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to calm and protect the delicate skin around the eyes. They help to reduce redness and swelling, while also promoting the healing of damaged tissues. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to alleviate eye irritation, making it a popular choice for those seeking a chemical-free solution to soothe and comfort their eyes.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds found in Calendula officinalis help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly caffeic acid and ferulic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the eyes from irritation.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Calendula officinalis, such as quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to calm and heal irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Employed for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: Utilized for their emollient and moisturizing properties to help soothe and hydrate dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when the petals are fully open and the sun is shining.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 50g of dried calendula flowers in 1 liter of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 2-4 weeks.
Mix 20g of the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye irritation.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The antimicrobial agents in eyebright creams, including terpenes and volatile oils, prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause eye infections and exacerbate irritation.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Eyebright creams often contain soothing herbs like aloe vera and chamomile, which help calm dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with eye irritation while also moisturizing and protecting the eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant's extracts contain tannins, flavonoids, and saponins, which work together to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. These active compounds also possess antimicrobial properties, helping to combat underlying infections that may be causing the irritation. As a result, Euphrasia officinalis creams can provide quick relief and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for eye irritation.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe eye irritation by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid found in Euphrasia officinalis, acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce swelling.

Tannins: Tannins in Euphrasia officinalis help to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation by constricting blood vessels and preventing the release of histamine, which can exacerbate eye inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their potent flavonoid and iridoid content, which helps to calm and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
Stems: The stems are used because they contain a significant amount of tannins and phenolic compounds that have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Infuse 10 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks of maceration.
Mix 10 milliliters of the strained oil with 90 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of beeswax for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Emollient and Soothing Properties: Marshmallow cream's emollient and soothing properties help to calm and relieve dry, itchy, and irritated eyes by creating a protective barrier that locks in moisture and reduces inflammation.

Anti-Inflammatory Activity: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in marshmallow creams, such as saponins and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain.

Mucilage Formation: Marshmallow cream's mucilages (thick, protective gel-like substances) form a protective film over the affected area, shielding it from further irritation and inflammation, while also helping to flush out any debris or allergens that may be contributing to the eye irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with eye irritation because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the marshmallow plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation. When applied topically to the eyes, these mucilages help to calm and comfort the area, providing relief from redness, itching, and burning sensations associated with eye irritation, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and calm the eye area, reducing inflammation and irritation by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
Mucilages: These thick, gel-like substances aid in soothing and moisturizing the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort by drawing out irritants and excess fluids.
Althaea saponins: These natural saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm and reduce inflammation in the eye area, thus providing relief from irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are commonly used because they contain high concentrations of mucilages, which provide soothing and protective properties for irritated eyes.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, glycosides, and mucilages, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Barks: Althaea officinalis barks are used because they contain mucilages and other compounds that help to calm and protect the delicate tissues around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis from a reliable source for medicinal use.
Infuse 10 grams of the dried root in 100 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion to remove the solids and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix 20 grams of emulsifying wax with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the liquid extract with the melted wax mixture and 50 milliliters of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lavender, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and damage to the eyes.
Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's relaxing and calming properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye irritation, and promote relaxation of the ocular muscles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with eye irritation because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. They also have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort associated with eye irritation. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide gentle, effective relief from eye irritations. It promotes a healthy recovery, allowing the eyes to heal naturally.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene ester has a calming effect on the skin and eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dryness or allergic reactions.
Kaempferol: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make creams for eye irritation because they contain calming and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.

Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make creams for eye irritation because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce redness and promote healing.

Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make creams for eye irritation because they contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to calm and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 lb of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them for 2 weeks.
Infuse 2 cups of dried flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of candelilla wax to the infused oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes then pour into molds to cool.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal tea creams containing herbs like chamomile, calendula, or peppermint help reduce inflammation in the eyes, soothing redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Herbs like green tea, hibiscus, or rosehip contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental factors.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Herbal teas rich in mucilages (such as marshmallow root or licorice root) create a protective barrier on the eye surface, providing long-lasting relief from dryness, itchiness, and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with eye irritation because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in Camellia sinensis have been shown to reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from irritated eyes. The natural moisturizing properties of the cream also help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort. This makes Camellia sinensis cream an effective solution for soothing eye irritation and promoting healthy eye skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye irritation by neutralizing free radicals and protecting the delicate tissues around the eyes.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.

Theaflavins: These unique flavonoids have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help shield the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting faster recovery and relief from eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: They are utilized for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce redness and discomfort associated with eye irritation.
Buds: They are chosen for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing eye irritation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused camellia sinensis liquid with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maiden hair tree extracts have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in maiden hair tree creams neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, which may contribute to dryness, itchiness, and redness.
Moisturizing: Maiden hair tree extracts are rich in fatty acids and other lipids that help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes, reducing the risk of further irritation and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract from the Ginkgo biloba leaves contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to soothe and calm the eyes. The cream may help reduce redness and swelling, while also protecting the eyes from environmental stressors and oxidative damage. As a natural remedy, Ginkgo biloba creams can provide relief from dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, promoting comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation.
Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects, which can help soothe and calm eye irritation.
Polymethylsiloxane (a type of saponin): These natural compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the eyes from further irritation and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba in eye care products due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: The buds are used in eye creams because they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids than the leaves, which helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds are used in eye creams due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which helps to alleviate eye irritation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a coffee grinder until they become a fine powder.
Mix 100 grams of sweet almond oil with 50 grams of jojoba oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax in a small saucepan.
Heat the wax mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it reaches 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Stir 5 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder into the oil mixture and pour it into a clean container.

remedy_properties: Cooled and Soothed: Peppermint's natural cooling effect helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes, providing immediate relief from redness and itchiness.

Anti-Inflammatory: The menthol in peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness around the eyes, making it an effective treatment for conjunctivitis and other eye infections.

Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage ocular tissues and contribute to eye irritation, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of chronic eye problems.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with eye irritation because of its natural anti-inflammatory and cooling properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams helps to reduce swelling and ease redness in the eyes. It also has a soothing effect on the skin around the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. This makes peppermint creams an effective remedy for relieving eye irritation caused by allergies, dryness, or exposure to irritants. Its natural composition makes it a popular choice for eye care.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps with eye irritation by providing a cooling sensation that reduces inflammation and soothes the eyes.
Linalool: It helps with eye irritation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that calm and protect the eyes from further irritation.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps with eye irritation by displaying potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that shield the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain a high amount of menthol, which makes them effective in reducing inflammation and providing relief from eye irritation.
Roots: The roots of the plant have a higher concentration of menthone, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh leaves and flowers from mature plants in late morning when essential oil content is highest.
Combine the fresh plant material with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and stir well for 5 minutes.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to release the herbal essence.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids after squeezing out excess oil.
Blend the extracted oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of emulsifying wax in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort contains anti-inflammatory compounds like hyperforin, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes caused by irritation.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort cream, such as flavonoids, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye irritation and promote tissue repair.

Soothing and calming effects: The soothing and calming properties of St. John's Wort cream can help alleviate itching, burning, and dryness associated with eye irritation, providing relief from discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that reduces inflammation and soothes the eyes. The cream also contains flavonoids, which help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. As a result, St. John's Wort creams can provide relief from redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with eye irritation, promoting a sense of calm and comfort in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
N-hydroxyhyperforin: This bioactive compound has been shown to exhibit strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help reduce eye irritation and promote healing.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help reduce eye redness and discomfort associated with irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes.
Roots: Contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help calm and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 20 minutes.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer for 10 minutes until it reaches a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with eye irritation because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it reduces swelling and redness associated with irritated eyes. Additionally, licorice extract has been shown to soothe and calm dry, itchy, and scratchy eyes. The cream's emollient properties also help to moisturize the eye surface, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Soothes and calms irritated eyes by reducing inflammation and relieving itchiness, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Licoricidin: Protects the eyes from further irritation by forming a barrier on the surface of the eye, preventing allergens and irritants from penetrating deeper tissues.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and damage, promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and healing irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil into a double boiler setup.
Melt the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for approximately 30 minutes until fully liquefied.
Add 20ml of distilled water and stir well to combine with the melted mixture for about 10 seconds.
Infuse 10g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into the liquid mixture by heating it gently for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool until it reaches a temperature suitable for pouring into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and redness alleviates discomfort and itching, providing instant relief. Burdock root contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, further protecting the delicate eye tissue from damage caused by environmental toxins, smoke, or other irritants. By applying this cream, individuals can enjoy a more comfortable and healthy eyesight.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Burdock cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like lignans, which help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock cream, such as polyphenols, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.
Soften and moisturize: Burdock cream's emollient properties help to soften and moisturize the skin around the eyes, reducing dryness and itchiness that can exacerbate eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of burdock contain inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain essential fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, which moisturizes and lubricates the eyes, reducing inflammation and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried arctium lapa leaves and stems measure one cup of plant material.
Combine the dry herb with two cups of distilled water in a heat-proof container steep for four hours.
Strain the liquid mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter collect one cup of herbal infusion.
Mix the herbal infusion with three tablespoons of beeswax and six tablespoons of coconut oil melt the mixture over low heat.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers allow to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with eye irritation because they contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's natural ingredients, such as plantain leaf extract, work to reduce redness and swelling while also combating bacterial infections that can cause eye discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of plantain creams help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchiness, dryness, and burning sensations associated with eye irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye irritation.
Antioxidant: Plantain's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye irritation and inflammation.

Soothing and calming: The mucilages present in plantain have a soothing and calming effect on the skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from itching, burning, and stinging associated with eye irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants and tannins, barks provide a natural way to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh plantago major leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Blend the leaves into a fine paste using 1/4 cup of cold-pressed olive oil for 10 minutes.
Mix the paste with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, heating until melted after 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any solids and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Transfer the cream to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy, scratchy, or burning sensations. Additionally, dandelion's natural astringent properties help to tighten the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. As a result, herbal dandelion creams offer a gentle and effective solution for soothing irritated eyes, promoting overall eye health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation, providing relief from conditions like conjunctivitis.

Antioxidant: Dandelion's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and damage, helping to protect the eyes from oxidative stress.

Soothing and emollient properties: Dandelion's soothing and emollient properties, courtesy of its polysaccharides and triterpenoid saponins, help calm and moisturize the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort caused by conditions like blepharitis or dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness associated with eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves in the early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the collected taraxacum officinale leaves using a food dehydrator at 65°c for 24 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Grind 50 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine the powdered taraxacum officinale with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with the herb's active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and store it at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate it for longer storage, allowing it to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The triterpenoids present in red clover have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, providing relief from itchy, red, and irritated eyes. Additionally, the cream's natural soothing properties help calm and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by allergens, dry air, or environmental factors. This makes herbal red clover creams a popular natural remedy for relieving eye irritation and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with dry eye syndrome and other eye irritations.

Antioxidant properties: Red clover's antioxidants help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and cataracts, two common causes of vision loss in older adults.

Soothing and calming effects: The topical application of red clover cream can provide immediate relief for eye irritation by soothing and calming the affected area, reducing redness and itching associated with conditions such as conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing triterpenoids and coumarins, these compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making them effective against eye irritants.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trifolium pratense flowers at peak blooming stage then dry them thoroughly in shade.
Combine dried trifolium pratense flowers with coconut oil and beeswax in double boiler mixture ratio of 10:5:3.
Heat mixture over low heat stirring occasionally for 30 minutes to ensure thorough infusion and dissolution.
Strain infused mixture through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow cream to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring to airtight containers and storing in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with eye irritation because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the eyes. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds in sage cream reduce redness, puffiness, and itchiness caused by dryness, allergies, or irritants. Additionally, sage's natural antibacterial and antifungal agents help eliminate any underlying infections or fungus that may be contributing to eye irritation. As a result, sage creams provide fast and effective relief from itchy, irritated eyes, allowing for comfortable vision and a reduced risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, reducing irritation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols present in sage have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with eye irritation, such as conjunctivitis or dry eyes.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, including camphor and bornyl acetate, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause eye infections, reducing the risk of complications and promoting a healthy ocular environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered salvia officinalis with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl, stirring well for 3 minutes to combine.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes until melted and smooth.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into sterilized glass jars, leaving about 1 inch of headspace for expansion during cooling.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before using or storing in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with eye irritation because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The natural oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce redness and swelling, providing fast relief from discomfort and itchiness. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat infection-causing bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy eye environment and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, exhibits antibacterial activity against a wide range of bacteria, including those that can cause eye infections and irritations, helping to prevent infection and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme contains flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with eye irritation, making it a natural remedy for conjunctivitis and other inflammatory eye conditions.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds in thyme, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage ocular tissues and exacerbate eye irritation, promoting overall eye health and protecting against age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing eye irritation.
Seeds: Thyme seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help calm inflammation and reduce redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried thyme leaves measuring one teaspoon per four ounces of carrier oil.
Combine the thyme leaves with sweet almond oil and coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture using a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for thirty minutes.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding solids.
Whip the infused oil mixture with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth creamy consistency taking approximately one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with eye irritation because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredient, valerian root extract, soothes and calms irritated eyes by reducing redness and puffiness. It also provides a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, shielding it from further irritation caused by allergens, smoke, or dry air. As a result, herbal valerian creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and damage to the eyes, reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in valerian cream reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation, providing relief from discomfort and promoting faster healing.
Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm irritated eyes by reducing inflammation, relaxing the eyelids, and soothing the surrounding skin, which can become inflamed or itchy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of valepotriates, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Dry valeriana officinalis herbals thoroughly at room temperature for three days to reduce moisture content.
Grind dried valeriana officinalis herbals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine 20 grams of ground valeriana officinalis with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Place the mixture in a double boiler setup over low heat for one hour to decant and infuse the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids and store the cream at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock creams helps with eye irritation because they contain soothing properties that calm and comfort the eyes. The creamy texture provides a gentle barrier, shielding the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and allergens. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds in curly dock help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with irritated eyes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties keep the eye area hydrated, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort. As a result, curly dock creams offer effective relief for dry, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Curly dock cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with eye irritation by decreasing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in curly dock cream, including vitamins C and E, neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to irritation, promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing and emollient properties: Curly dock's mucilages and saponins provide a protective barrier on the surface of the skin, soothing and calming irritated eyes while also moisturizing and lubricating the conjunctiva.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: Roots are employed due to their rich content of tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide a protective barrier for the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including fresh rumex crispus leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and distilled water.
Sterilize the equipment and workspace by washing with soap and rinsing thoroughly under running water.
Prepare a decoction of 20g fresh rumex crispus leaves in 200ml distilled water for 15 minutes to extract its bioactive compounds.
Melt 30g coconut oil and 10g beeswax in a heat-proof bowl over low heat, stirring occasionally until fully incorporated and smooth.
Combine the cooled decoction with the melted oil mixture and stir well before pouring into sterilized containers for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye irritation are topical treatments made from natural herbs that provide relief from redness, itching, and dryness associated with eye irritation. They work by soothing and calming the skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Examples of herbal creams include aloe vera, chamomile, and green tea, which help to alleviate eye irritation and improve sleep quality, vision, and overall well-being, enhancing daily life and productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye irritation that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. They can also be used to treat minor burns, cuts, and scrapes by providing a barrier against infection and promoting wound closure.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with eye irritation is a blend of Aloe Vera, Chamomile, and Green Tea. Aloe Vera soothes and calms the eyes, reducing inflammation and redness. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. Green Tea, rich in antioxidants, protects the eyes from further irritation and promotes healing. Together, these creams create a powerful and natural remedy to alleviate eye irritation and promote healthy, comfortable eyes.
